speaker: Travis
text: Alright, let's start the standup. I want to talk through the import feature today since we just shipped the transcript parser.

speaker: Priya
text: Sounds good. I had one concern looking at the diff — the snapshot mode field. We're saving imports as mode 'live', right?

speaker: Travis
text: Yeah. The reasoning is the captured shape matches a live meeting — graph, transcript, thoughts, all the same. The new 'source' field is what distinguishes the origin. That way the meeting browser doesn't have to special-case anything.

speaker: Priya
text: Makes sense. Does the browser surface the source anywhere yet?

speaker: Travis
text: Not yet. The data is on disk so we can add a badge later. Out of scope for this ticket, in the next one if anyone's bothered.

speaker: Sam
text: Speaking of next ones — what's the ETA on audio import? AIZ-31?

speaker: Travis
text: After we live with transcript import for a bit. Want to make sure the extraction loop holds up under offline pacing before adding the ASR layer on top.

speaker: Priya
text: One more thing — how do unlabeled chunks behave? Like a podcast transcript with no speaker tags.

speaker: Travis
text: They get speaker 'unknown'. The re-evaluation architecture from AIZ-9 handles solo monologue fine, so a podcast should produce a coherent graph. We can revisit if real podcasts surface edge cases.

speaker: Travis
text: Code-signing for macOS is still open. Not blocking import work, but users who download from the landing page still see the quarantine warning. Different ticket, AIZ-29.

speaker: Priya
text: OK. I'll write up a one-pager on the badge UX so we're ready when we want to ship it.
